description: The Dojin-kai is a yakuza organization headquartered in Kurume, Fukuoka, Japan, founded in 1971 by Isoji Koga. It is a designated yakuza syndicate with approximately 310 members, known for its militant nature and large-scale drug trafficking, particularly methamphetamine. The group has been involved in violent conflicts with other yakuza groups, including the Yamaguchi-gumi and its own splinter group, Kyushu Seido-kai.

country_links.evidence_quote: This group had also smuggled methamphetamine from Taiwan where it had a connection with a local Taiwanese drug ring.
country_links.context: The Dojin-kai smuggled methamphetamine from Taiwan, where it had a connection with a local Taiwanese drug ring.
